## Public Boundaries

- [ ] Uses synthetic or public-safe examples only.
- [ ] Adds no private Hypernovelty material, customer/student data, credentials, tokens, account exports, private paths, grant strategy, or internal notes.
- [ ] Makes no claims of certification, adoption, compliance, medical/legal/financial advice, production readiness, search ranking, or institutional approval.
- [ ] Preserves human approval for public actions, account changes, money, legal, medical, schooling, customer data, deployments, and other high-stakes contexts.
- [ ] Adds no new standalone primitive unless it has passed the project triage rule.
